name: King Cobra
irwin_note: Steve had personal experience. Multiple times.
stevens_note: Has a spiritual connection with this animal specifically.
oshea_note: Has written the definitive paper on Ophiophagus feeding behaviour.

name: Black Mamba
irwin_note: Steve called this one by name before he got close to it.
stevens_note: The speed alone commands respect.
oshea_note: Two drops kills a human. Known this since university. Still here.

name: Puff Adder
irwin_note: Responsible for more African snakebite deaths than any other. Sits still. People step on it.
stevens_note: The slow ones are the most dangerous. A lesson for living.
oshea_note: Cytotoxic. The flesh dissolves. Antivenom often unavailable in time.

name: Brazilian Wandering Spider
irwin_note: Steve picked one up. Panel does not endorse this.
stevens_note: Do not move. Breathe slowly. It will make a decision.
oshea_note: Most venomous spider. Also causes priapism. This is not helpful when it is on your chest.

name: Box Jellyfish
irwin_note: Steve swam past these constantly. Never mentioned it.
stevens_note: It does not mean to harm you. It simply does.
oshea_note: Fastest kill of any marine animal. Tentacles continue stinging after detachment.

name: Great White Shark
irwin_note: Steve once swam with a white pointer. Did not mention it until asked.
stevens_note: I once swam with a white pointer. The eye sees everything.
oshea_note: Investigative bite. Usually one strike. Usually enough.
hales_note: Punching the nose is a myth. Poke the eye.

name: Saltwater Crocodile
irwin_note: Steve got in the water with one. On purpose. With a camera.
stevens_note: Ancient. Has no concept of you as anything other than prey.
oshea_note: Death roll. Internal organs detach before you understand what is happening.
hales_note: We ate one in 1987. Different context.

name: Grizzly Bear
irwin_note: Steve found bears polite by comparison to some things he had handled.
stevens_note: The mother-and-cubs scenario is the most predictable. Predict it.
oshea_note: Todd Orr was attacked twice by the same bear. Fox finds this tactically embarrassing.

name: Polar Bear
irwin_note: Steve never worked in the Arctic. This is notable.
stevens_note: Hunts humans as prey, not in defence. Different category entirely.
oshea_note: Only bear that actively hunts humans. No deterrent strategy works reliably.

name: Komodo Dragon
irwin_note: Steve described the Komodo as the one animal that made him think twice. Once.
stevens_note: The venom prevents the blood from clotting. It waits for you to stop moving.
oshea_note: Has the definitive academic paper on Varanus komodoensis venom glands. References it.
hales_note: Edible if prepared correctly. Not recommended in this circumstance.

name: Lion
irwin_note: Steve had enormous respect for lions. Also went near them.
stevens_note: A pride is a committee. Committees make decisions slowly. Use this.
oshea_note: Most lion attacks are from injured or aging males. No comfort in the statistics.
hales_note: Noise and size. Stand your ground.

name: Spotted Hyena (pack)
irwin_note: Steve noted the matriarchal structure with evident admiration.
stevens_note: The laugh is communication. When they stop laughing, the decision has been made.
oshea_note: Bite force exceeds lions. Bone-crunching jaw structure. Attenborough narrates this well.

name: Hippopotamus
irwin_note: Steve considered hippos the most dangerous animal in Africa. Unusual position for him.
stevens_note: The aggression is territorial, not predatory. This distinction does not help you.
oshea_note: Responsible for more African human fatalities than any other large mammal. Not a fun fact.

name: Cape Buffalo
irwin_note: Steve kept a respectful distance. This is notable.
stevens_note: Known as the Black Death. Called that for a reason.
oshea_note: Will return to finish the job after initial charge. Unlike most animals. Notable.
hales_note: Do not climb a small tree. They will wait.

name: Cassowary
irwin_note: Steve handled one in his early career. Footage exists.
stevens_note: The claw. Single kick. You will not see it.
oshea_note: Most dangerous bird on Earth. Dagger claw 12cm. Disembowelment is documented.

name: Bull Elephant (musth)
irwin_note: Steve gave charging elephants considerably more room than charging crocodiles.
stevens_note: In musth, the hormone levels are beyond reason. Reason will not help you here.
oshea_note: Temporal gland secretion means musth. Musth means do not approach. At all.

name: Moose
irwin_note: Steve found moose temperamentally unpredictable. More dangerous than they looked.
stevens_note: Kills more people in North America than wolves, bears, and cougars combined. People do not know this.
oshea_note: Trample mechanism. Not a predator. Does not stop when you fall.

name: Killer Whale
irwin_note: Steve had respect. Mutual.
stevens_note: No documented wild orca fatality. The captivity incidents are different. Different entirely.
oshea_note: Wild: essentially no risk. Captive: significant risk. This distinction matters for the panel.

name: Portuguese Man o' War
irwin_note: Steve swam past many. Occasionally brushed one. Did not stop filming.
stevens_note: Not a jellyfish. A colony. Each part a separate organism with a specific role. Attenborough has things to say.
oshea_note: Tentacles extend 50 metres. Detached tentacles sting for hours. Urinating on it does not help.

name: Manatee
irwin_note: Steve loved manatees. Quietly. Without the usual energy.
stevens_note: The manatee did not intend this. The manatee never intends anything.
oshea_note: The mechanism of injury requires explanation and is funnier than the injury.
